title: Hands-on Experiment on the Design of qPCR Primers for the Detection of Mycotoxigenic Fungi
author: Roumani, Foteini
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This presentation is part of the training material of FunShield4Med 3rd Seminar entitled "The Occurrence of Non-Regulated Mycotoxins in Foods" held at Institute of Technology of Agricultural Products on 21 March 2024. This workshop-style presentation guides participants through designing qPCR primers targeting fungal genes involved in mycotoxin biosynthesis—specifically the nor-1 gene for aflatoxins. It outlines a step-by-step workflow: from gene sequence retrieval and primer optimization to amplicon design and validation. Tools like Primer3, Geneious, and various genome browsers are used for sequence selection, while best practices for primer and probe design ensure specificity, sensitivity, and efficiency. The session emphasizes the importance of in silico analysis before lab testing and provides links to trusted vendors for primer synthesis.
